text:
On a sunny Saturday morning, Jack wakes up feeling a bit peckish. He decides to go for a walk down to his favorite little cafe. As he meanders through the park, eating a delightful honeyed oatmeal muffin, he spots Grandma Betty at the crosswalk, pushing her wheelchair.

She greets him enthusiastically.

"Oh hi there, Jack! You're as sharp as ever. Have you had your breakfast?"

"I’ve had it," Jack laughs.

"Good choice today. You’ll need the energy.”

They chat about nothing important for a while before Grandma Betty’s granddaughter Amelia joins them.

Amelia loves hearing stories. She’s always full of questions.

"Grandma Betty, do all chickens lay eggs?

Grandma Betty laughs.

"Oh honey, some chickens don't lay eggs. They're birds."

Amelia nods thoughtfully.

"What does a hen lay then?" Amelia asks eagerly.

Grandma Betty smiles knowingly.

"A hen lays an egg a day," she says.

Am
